mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels is negative over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur less than 50% of the time when rates are below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer spending, triggering negative equity returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of structural unemployment shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and slowing economic momentum, leading to negative equity returns as investors price in lower growth.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 25bps in a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI exceeding 3.0% annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 60% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, supporting positive equity market performance over the following quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in < 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while housing starts decelerate signals a shift in demand composition away from interest-sensitive sectors, causing negative returns in broad equity indices.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when GDPC1 growth outpaces HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average indicates a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases when INDPRO drops below its 12-month average, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of high volatility.
